function usage() {
  console.log(`
  Grasp — codebase architecture analyser

  Usage:
    npx grasp [path]           Analyse a local directory (default: .)
    npx grasp owner/repo       Analyse a GitHub repo
    npx grasp <url>            Analyse a GitHub URL

  Options:
    --json                     Print full JSON report to stdout
    --out <file>               Write JSON report to file (default: grasp-report.json)
    --help                     Show this help

  Environment:
  // ...

issues.title: 2 Unused Functions
issues.desc: Functions not called from other files

patterns.name: Singleton
patterns.desc: Ensures a class has only one instance. Common for configuration, logging, or connection pools.

patterns.name: Factory
patterns.desc: Creates objects without specifying exact class. Enables loose coupling and extensibility.

patterns.name: Observer/Event
patterns.desc: Defines a subscription mechanism for event-driven architecture. Great for decoupling.

patterns.name: Context Provider
patterns.desc: React Context for global state. Alternative to prop drilling.

patterns.name: God Object
patterns.desc: Files with too many responsibilities (15+ functions). Consider splitting into smaller modules.

patterns.name: Long File
patterns.desc: Files over 500 lines are harder to maintain. Consider breaking into smaller modules.

patterns.name: VBA God Module
patterns.desc: VBA modules with 20+ procedures. Consider splitting into smaller modules.

security.title: XSS Vulnerability
security.desc: Direct HTML injection can lead to XSS attacks. Sanitize user input.

security.title: Shell Command Execution
security.desc: Shell() executes system commands. Ensure input is validated.

security.title: Dynamic Code Execution
security.desc: eval() executes arbitrary code. Avoid if possible or validate input strictly.

security.title: Function Constructor
